name: Vegetable-Stuffed Omelets/Ginger Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp pineapple juice or orange juice
1/2 tsp grated ginger root (or 1/8 teaspoon ground ginger)
Optional: toasted walnuts or almonds, shredded radish, thinly sliced cucumber
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare vegetables by cutting asparagus into 3-inch lengths, carrots into 3-inch sticks, and green onions into 2-inch lengths. Shred radishes and slice cucumbers (optional).
2. In a large saucepan, cook asparagus, carrots, and green onions in a small amount of boiling, lightly salted water for 7-9 minutes until crisp-tender. Drain well.
3. Meanwhile, stir together sweet-sour sauce, pineapple juice or orange juice, and grated ginger root (or ground ginger) in a bowl.
4. In a small bowl, combine eggs and water. Beat until combined but not frothy.
5. Heat 1 teaspoon of oil in an 8-10 inch skillet with flared sides over medium heat until a drop of water sizzles.
6. Add the egg mixture to the skillet and cook for 30 seconds to 1 minute, or until set but still shiny. Use a spatula to lift the edges and allow uncooked egg to flow underneath.
7. When the eggs are set but still shiny, use the spatula to transfer them onto warm plates. Cover with plastic wrap.
8. Repeat with remaining egg mixture and oil as needed to make 2 omelets.
9. Spread some of the sauce onto each omelet. Arrange steamed vegetables on one quarter of each omelet, fanning vegetables toward the edge.
10. Fold each omelet over the vegetables, then fold again. Top with additional sauce and sprinkle with toasted walnuts or almonds.
